habitat: Deciduous/open forest; dry semi-desert grasslands; scrub; agricultural land; and areas near water
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Sri Lanka Indian Peafowl (Pavo cristatus). Classified under the Phasianidae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Deciduous/open forest; dry semi-desert grasslands; scrub; agricultural land; and areas near water. The current IUCN classification of Least Concern (LC) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Sri Lanka Indian Peafowl is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Sri Lanka Indian Peafowl, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
